name: Simons Simplex Collection
sample_size: Approximately 2,600 simplex families (one affected child, unaffected parents, 80%+ with unaffected sibling); WGS from 6,383 individuals across 2,274 families as of SFARI_SSC_WGS_2a release

family_structure.definition: One child with autism spectrum disorder (ASD), unaffected parents, unaffected sibling when available

genome_coverage.complementary_data: Whole exome sequencing also available for subset

key_publications.title: The Simons Simplex Collection: A Resource for Identification of Autism Genetic Risk Factors
key_publications.relevance: Original SSC resource paper describing family structure and phenotyping

key_publications.title: The contribution of de novo coding mutations to autism spectrum disorder
key_publications.relevance: Landmark study showing 30% of simplex cases driven by de novo mutations; genome-wide discovery

key_publications.title: Insights into Autism Spectrum Disorder Genomic Architecture and Biology from 71 Risk Loci
key_publications.relevance: TADA analysis combining SSC and ASC data; definitive list of high-confidence autism genes

key_publications.title: Identifying associations of de novo noncoding variants with autism through integration of gene expression, sequence, and sex information
key_publications.relevance: Recent noncoding variant analysis from SSC WGS data

available_analyses: De novo mutation discovery (coding and noncoding)

noncoding_variant_data.note: WGS enables comprehensive noncoding variant detection not possible with WES

research_impact: Foundation for >100 publications on autism genetic architecture
distinctive_features: Simplex-only design is optimal for de novo variant discovery
Deepest phenotyping of any major autism cohort
Gold-standard resource for validating autism genes
Primary discovery resource for de novo mutations in autism
Foundation for most major autism gene discoveries 2010-2025
